name: Arkansas Health Center
description_short: Arkansas Health Center Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering savings accounts, loans, and online banking to employees with a common bond.
description_long:
Arkansas Health Center Federal Credit Union (AHCFCU) is a non-profit financial cooperative chartered and supervised by the National Credit Union Administration. Based in Benton, Arkansas, it operates as a member-owned institution rather than a profit-driven entity, making membership available as an employee benefit through employer partnerships. The credit union has been serving its membership since at least 2013.

AHCFCU offers a full range of consumer financial products including multiple savings account options, auto loans, personal loans, boat and RV financing, mortgage partnerships, and various insurance products. Members receive 24/7 online banking access through their website and voice response telephone system (1-855-538-2642). The institution partners with Servion Mortgage for residential mortgage origination and supports debit card services through MasterCard with dedicated after-hours lost/stolen card support.

As a credit union, AHCFCU differentiates itself through the non-profit cooperative model, which allows members to potentially benefit from lower interest rates on loans and higher dividends on savings compared to traditional banks. All member share accounts are federally insured up to $250,000 by the NCUA. The institution emphasizes its mission of "People Helping People" and positions itself as the "Preferred Financial Institution" for members seeking affordable, courteous financial services.

AHCFCU is a straightforward, traditional credit union with limited geographic presence (single location in Benton, AR with restricted hours) and no indication of specialized services or competitive advantages beyond standard credit union offerings. The institution serves a specific membership base tied to Arkansas Health Center employment and does not appear to offer digital banking innovation, competitive rate promotions, or extensive branch/ATM networks typical of larger financial institutions.
services: Savings accounts with online banking access 24/7
Residential mortgage loans (through Servion Mortgage partnership)
MasterCard debit cards with 24/7 lost/stolen support
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure allows for potentially lower loan rates and higher savings dividends compared to traditional banks
Federal NCUA insurance covers share accounts up to $250,000, providing government-backed deposit protection
24/7 access to accounts through online banking and voice response telephone system at 1-855-538-2642
Diverse loan products including auto, boat, RV, personal, farm equipment, and residential mortgages through Servion partnership
Credit life insurance, GAP insurance, and credit disability insurance available to eligible members on loans
MasterCard debit card with dedicated 24/7 after-hours lost/stolen card support at 1-888-263-3370
Mission-driven "People Helping People" philosophy with focus on improving member financial lives
cons: Single physical location in Benton, AR with limited hours (closes at 4:00-4:30 p.m. weekdays, closed weekends)
Membership restricted to employees with a common bond through Arkansas Health Center employer affiliation
No information provided about competitive interest rates, fee schedules details, or digital banking features beyond basic online access
Limited online presence and outdated website design (copyright shows 2013-2026 but appears to be legacy platform)
No indication of ATM network access, mobile app availability, or modern fintech features
best_for: Arkansas Health Center employees seeking affordable financing through an employer-affiliated credit union
Members wanting federal deposit insurance and non-profit financial institution benefits
Consumers interested in auto, boat, RV, or personal loans with potentially lower rates than traditional banks
Long-term savers wanting member-owned institution benefits with NCUA insurance protection
diagnosis: AHCFCU is best suited for Arkansas Health Center employees seeking straightforward credit union banking with potential cost savings through the non-profit cooperative model and federal insurance protection. The primary caveat is membership restriction to employer-affiliated individuals and limited geographic/service accessibility compared to larger financial institutions, making it primarily valuable for those within the Arkansas Health Center employment network.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Arkansas Health Center — BENTON, AR Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Arkansas Health Center — NCUA-insured credit union in BENTON, AR. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
